ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges. Geronal Washington in Propria Persona.

Mr. Siracusa stated that the escrow statement has now been filed and he has a revised order to reflect Mr. Conrad's fees. Mr. Siracusa addressed the issue raised by the Court with regard to reimbursement to Geronal Washington without a Court order and reimbursement of an insurance premium when it was already paid stating that his Client believed it was justified and appropriate to reimburse Mr. Washington for the property taxes, that they were paid after the accounting period, that he is not asking for retroactive approval, that Mr. Washington paid the insurance but it was in his name and that Mr. Washington was told by Counsel that it needed to be in the name of the Trust. Mr. Washington stated that he paid the taxes in advance and has been since 2010, that once the sister was out of the house he could not continue to insure it, that he got coverage for earthquake and fire but could no coverage for people living in the house, and that he does not agree with the amount of $75,000.00 being held back for taxes. Mr. Siracusa explained that the 2009 taxes were paid and they are trying to get money back, the house was sold and that the $75,000.00 amount was for the capital gains issue. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order). The Court will not instruct the Trustee to pay duplicative insurance costs because the Trustee also paid it because he could not get proof from Mr. Washington.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for and with Sonia Washington. Melody Yasuda specially appearing for Attorney Lucy McAllister for and with Geronal Washington. Andrew Schultz for Scott Hodges (Trustee). Alice Dueker from the Beck Law Group.

The Court advised Ms. Dueker that her matter (issue of lien on any distribution to Sonia Washington) is to be taken up on the ex parte calendar at 10:00 a.m. Mr. Freedman expressed concern about his Client being extremely emotional about losing the house, asked that the matter go longer before selling the house, stated that he made a proposal to Ms. McAllister and spoke about mediation. Ms. Yasuda stated that they are opposed to mediaiton and that they would like the sale of the property to go through. Ms. Washington expressed her concerns.
